test2: Hi, I'm Jonathan, I am currently a first-year student of the Peip D program in Nantes. Passionate about computer science since a young age, i had the opportunity to explore different technologies. I started out by making games with Unity using C# which quickly sparked my interested in Object-Oriented Development. During my journey, I was fortunate enough to learn about data science which is the field I plan to pursue a career in. Whether it be by simple analyzes using Pandas and Matplotlib or tinkering with Pytorch, I never cease to be amazed by this field.
comp22: Whether it be to undertake new projects or face new challenges, I'm always energetic and operational !
comp32: Passionate about computer science, I always strive to give my best and suceed in every task I undertake.
comp42: Whatever the technology, I'll always find a way to contribute and learn new skills when needed !
Port2: Hover the cards for more information, click them to explore the project
Card12: Made with : Unreal Engine 5 using blueprint
Card22: Made with : Python and the PIL library
Card42: Made with : Unity programmed in C#, 3D models created with Blender, networking framework : Fishnet
Card52: Made with : Python and the pandas and matplotlib libraries
Card1Tex: The point of that project was to use an algorithm to create some perlin noise (proccedural texture) and generate terrain with height variation using that texture.
Card2T: Projet : Pixel arrangement Andy Warhol style
Card2Tex: The point of that project was to learn to manipulate the pixels of an image using Python and the Python Image Library
Card3Tex: The point of this Go lang programmed school project was to generate a great number of particles and manage their evolution. A true optimzation challenge spiced up with some mathematical algorithm used to generate the system under a specific form such as the circle system shown below.
Card4T: Projet : Multiplayer game with network-calculated physics
Card4Tex: This project using Unity was meant to be a challenge to me by forcing me to broaden my game development knowledge by using a networking framework thus having to simulate physics on the server side
Card5Tex: This statistics driven project had the objective to compare different trading strategies and compare them to a simple investment in ETFs in order to calculate risks and volatility in modern trading
